ideation: This sequence mimics the action of applying an adhesive bandage.  The animation focuses on one arm, using a combination of joint movements to create a natural-looking application gesture. The arm starts in a neutral position, then moves to pick up the bandage, positions it, and finally presses it into place. The other arm remains largely static to maintain balance and focus on the action. Frame 1: The arm starts in a relaxed position at the side, ready to move. Frame 2: The arm moves forward and slightly upward, as if reaching for the bandage. Frame 3: The arm moves downward and slightly inward, positioning the bandage. Frame 4: The arm presses the bandage into place with a slight downward and inward movement. Frame 5: The arm returns to a resting position.

frames.beat: arms rest at sides, ready for action
the right arm reaches forward, preparing to grasp
the right arm moves to position the bandage
the right arm presses the bandage firmly in place
the right arm returns to a relaxed resting position
